chapter_context.summary: Elijah the Tishbite declares a drought in Israel; he is miraculously fed by ravens at Cherith Brook, multiplies meal and oil for the widow of Zarephath, and raises her young son from the dead.

outline.text: Elijah Prophesies Drought; Hidden by Cherith Brook; Fed by Ravens
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Prophesies Drought; Hidden by Cherith Brook; Fed by Ravens (17:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah and the Widow of Zarephath; Miracle of Meal and Oil
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah and the Widow of Zarephath; Miracle of Meal and Oil (17:8-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Raises the Widow's Son from Death
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Raises the Widow's Son from Death (17:17-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 17, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
